paper.title: Review See-Through: Transparent Evidence Layers for Paper Review Agents

claims.text: On the archived five-case harness, Review See-Through classified review evidence layers with perfect (1.0) layer accuracy across 15 criticisms.
claims.rationale: Both the abstract and the numbered claim explicitly state 1.0 layer accuracy across 15 criticisms on the five-case harness.

claims.text: On the same archived harness, Review See-Through selected the target claim with perfect (1.0) accuracy and caught all off-scope criticisms (1.0 catch rate).
claims.rationale: The abstract sentence and Claim 2 both report 1.0 target-claim accuracy and 1.0 off-scope catch rate on the archived harness.

claims.text: On the archived harness, the see-through next-experiment selector achieved 1.0 precision versus 0.4 for the raw-review baseline, an absolute gain of 0.6.
claims.rationale: The abstract and Claim 3 state the precision values and 0.6 gain; a limitation note clarifies this comes from a five-case harness but does not contradict the measured result.

claims.text: On the three-case real extraction evaluation, Review See-Through achieved perfect (1.0) accuracy/recall/precision for claim-status, evidence references, limitations, and selected targets.
claims.rationale: The abstract and Claims 4–5 together state 1.0 claim-status accuracy, evidence-reference recall/precision, limitation recall, and selected-target accuracy on a three-case evaluation; a limitation notes the evaluation scope but not the metrics themselves.

claims.text: On the existing labeled harnesses, the see-through decomposition preserves review structure, selects review targets, and improves next-experiment selection.
claims.rationale: The abstract explicitly says these results support this narrow claim about preserving structure, selecting targets, and improving next-experiment selection on the existing labeled harnesses.

claims.text: Review See-Through will maintain at least 0.9 next-experiment precision on a larger cross-domain paper set of at least 20 cases.
claims.rationale: Claim 6 is explicitly forward-looking and marked as needing an experiment; the limitations explicitly say this cross-domain precision claim is untested and the current strongest result comes from only five cases.

evidence.text: In the archived five-case harness, the layer classifier reached 1.0 layer accuracy, 1.0 target-claim accuracy, and 1.0 off-scope catch rate across 15 criticisms.
The same harness measured 1.0 precision for the see-through next-experiment selector versus 0.4 for the raw review baseline, a 0.6 absolute gain.
A separate three-case extraction evaluation measured 1.0 accuracy or recall/precision for claim counts, claim statuses, evidence references, limitations, and selected targets.

These results support a narrow claim: on the existing labeled harnesses, the see-through decomposition preserves review structure, selects review targets, and improves next-experiment selection, while downstream revision quality, broader paper domains, and adversarial reviews remain untested.
1. On the archived five-case harness, Review See-Through classified review evidence layers with 1.0 layer accuracy across 15 criticisms.
2. On the same archived harness, Review See-Through selected the target claim with 1.0 accuracy and caught off-scope criticisms at a 1.0 rate.
3. On the archived harness, the see-through next-experiment selector reached 1.0 precision while the raw-review baseline reached 0.4 precision, giving a 0.6 absolute gain.
4. On the three-case real extraction evaluation, the system achieved 1.0 claim-status accuracy, 1.0 evidence-reference recall, and 1.0 evidence-reference precision.
5. On the three-case real extraction evaluation, Review See-Through achieved 1.0 limitation recall and 1.0 selected-target accuracy.
6. Review See-Through will maintain at least 0.9 next-experiment precision on a larger cross-domain paper set of at least 20 cases (needs experiment).
- The strongest next-experiment result comes from a five-case archived harness, so the measured gain may not hold on larger or more diverse paper sets.
- The extraction evaluation covers three cases and reports parser-facing metrics rather than downstream author revision quality.
- The untested cross-domain precision claim is intentionally marked as needing an experiment and should not be treated as established evidence.

limitations.text: These results support a narrow claim: on the existing labeled harnesses, the see-through decomposition preserves review structure, selects review targets, and improves next-experiment selection, while downstream revision quality, broader paper domains, and adversarial reviews remain untested.
- The strongest next-experiment result comes from a five-case archived harness, so the measured gain may not hold on larger or more diverse paper sets.
- The extraction evaluation covers three cases and reports parser-facing metrics rather than downstream author revision quality.
- The untested cross-domain precision claim is intentionally marked as needing an experiment and should not be treated as established evidence.

filtered_criticisms.text: The strong reported next-experiment precision gain is based on a very small, homogeneous evaluation setup, which makes it hard to judge how robust this improvement would be on more varied or noisy real-world reviews and papers.
filtered_criticisms.quote: - The strongest next-experiment result comes from a five-case archived harness, so the measured gain may not hold on larger or more diverse paper sets.
filtered_criticisms.hidden_assumption: Performance measured on a five-case archived harness is sufficiently representative of broader, more diverse paper and review distributions to substantiate a strong claim about improved next-experiment selection.
filtered_criticisms.filter_reason: off_scope: Drop. The criticism essentially restates an acknowledged limitation without adding a new check. The paper already says, verbatim, "The strongest next-experiment result comes from a five-case archived harness, so the measured gain may not hold on larger or more diverse paper sets." The concern about robustness to "more varied or noisy real-world reviews and papers" is the same point as "larger or more diverse paper sets," so this does not introduce additional analysis beyond what the authors already concede.

filtered_criticisms.text: The paper’s central narrative that the decomposition preserves review structure and helps select targets is evaluated only via parser-facing metrics on small harnesses, without any evidence that this translates into improved downstream author revision quality or end-to-end review usefulness.
filtered_criticisms.quote: These results support a narrow claim: on the existing labeled harnesses, the see-through decomposition preserves review structure, selects review targets, and improves next-experiment selection, while downstream revision quality, broader paper domains, and adversarial reviews remain untested.
filtered_criticisms.hidden_assumption: High accuracy on claim, evidence, and limitation extraction metrics on small harnesses implies that real authors and reviewers will experience meaningfully better revision outcomes or review workflows.
filtered_criticisms.filter_reason: off_scope: Drop. The paper explicitly narrows its claims and flags exactly this gap: "These results support a narrow claim: on the existing labeled harnesses, the see-through decomposition preserves review structure, selects review targets, and improves next-experiment selection, while downstream revision quality, broader paper domains, and adversarial reviews remain untested." The criticism that evaluation is limited to parser-facing metrics and does not show "improved downstream author revision quality or end-to-end review usefulness" is directly acknowledged in that sentence. Since the authors already restrict their claims accordingly, this criticism just rephrases an admitted limitation without adding a concrete new check or pointing out an inconsistency.

filtered_criticisms.text: The forward-looking cross-domain precision claim is purely speculative at this point and would benefit from at least an outline of the planned experimental design or any preliminary evidence that the method can scale beyond the current five-case harness.
filtered_criticisms.quote: 6. Review See-Through will maintain at least 0.9 next-experiment precision on a larger cross-domain paper set of at least 20 cases (needs experiment).
filtered_criticisms.hidden_assumption: The behavior and precision of the next-experiment selector observed on the existing five-case harness will largely carry over to a larger, cross-domain setting of at least 20 cases, despite the authors not yet having run such an experiment.
filtered_criticisms.filter_reason: off_scope: Drop. Claim C6 is explicitly labeled as speculative and out of current empirical scope: "Review See-Through will maintain at least 0.9 next-experiment precision on a larger cross-domain paper set of at least 20 cases (needs experiment)." The limitations further state that "The untested cross-domain precision claim is intentionally marked as needing an experiment and should not be treated as established evidence." The criticism notes that the claim is speculative and asks for an outline of planned experiments or preliminary evidence; that is future-work guidance rather than a valid criticism of the present paper, which already brackets this as untested. It does not uncover any overclaiming relative to the text, so it should be dropped.

selected_next_experiment.hypothesis: A bounded experiment can resolve whether the paper's evidence supports C6: Review See-Through will maintain at least 0.9 next-experiment precision on a larger cross-domain paper set of at least 20 cases.
selected_next_experiment.baseline: Use the closest reported evidence sentence as the baseline before running the missing check.
selected_next_experiment.keep_condition: claim-specific pass rate passes on a held-out fixture and the evidence can be reproduced from saved artifacts.
selected_next_experiment.discard_condition: claim-specific pass rate fails on the held-out fixture or requires evidence not present in the paper artifacts.
selected_next_experiment.verify: Run the smallest saved fixture that directly targets C6 and attach the run artifact.
